db_assertions: a fresh PALAI_HOME clean install came up on the production profile (compose.yaml + production.yml): the fail-closed production-entrypoint.sh admitted a REAL (non-dev, 32-byte-hex) secret master key, and the stack has no public self-registration surface — provisioning is bootstrap-key + the `palai` admin CLI only (master_key_non_dev + registration_closed)

the admin CLI + the run reached the control-plane THROUGH the self-signed TLS edge with CA verification (edge_verified) — not the loopback API; `palai config validate` (static production posture) and doctor v2 were green (config_valid + doctor_green)

restart-less install spine: the ten SPINE steps (clean-install, production-bring-up, tls-edge-verified, config-validate, doctor-v2, provision-tenant, real-run, metrics-probe, backup, support-bundle) resolved on ONE stack boot with restart_count=0 (proven live by pg_postmaster_start_time identical start-to-end); the restore into a SEPARATE stack is DR-002 (a second stack), not this spine. journey_digest = hashParts(canonical step_ids) is re-derivable + anchored (anti-fabrication)

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the local-production-compose seam on Docker Desktop with a self-minted local-CA edge cert. The dedicated cloud-VM clean install with a real domain/cert is the operator leg — named, NOT claimed

`palai backup` captured stack A (source_project palai-selfhost-a) into one archive — a consistent pg_dump -Fc + the object-store volume + a manifest of ids + per-object checksums (migration_version 32); the manifest holds NO secret

`palai restore` loaded that archive into a SEPARATE clean stack B (target_project palai-selfhost-b, distinct PALAI_HOME/ports/volumes) whose no-clobber empty-target gate refused nothing because B held no tenant data (target_was_empty) — restore into a non-empty target is fail-closed

manifest_digest = hashParts(kind, migration, org ids, sample response) is re-derivable from the declared canonical backup identity (tests/uat/self-host selfHostBackupManifestParts) — a fabricated digest is caught by tests/uat/self-host (the managed-cloud artifact-digest precedent)
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): two isolated production-compose stacks on ONE Docker Desktop host (the T4 pattern). Restore to a separate PHYSICAL host / cloud VM is the operator leg — named, NOT claimed

`palai restore verify` (integrity focus, DR-004): the archive's db + object-store members re-hashed to the manifest (archive_checksum), the restored live schema version matched the manifest (migration_version), every backed-up org id was present in the restored data (tenant_ids), and the manifest's sample response was queryable from the restored database (run_retrieval)
all six checks are reported together (the shipped InstallRestoreVerify runs them as one); this case pins the integrity + retrievability quarter
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): restore verify against the restored stack B on the local-production-compose seam; the cloud-VM restore is the operator leg — named, NOT claimed

`palai restore verify` (isolation focus, DR-005): tenant isolation SURVIVED the restore — every org-bearing table on the restored data still FORCE'd row-level security and carried its tenant_isolation policy (rls_isolation). pg_dump carries FORCE RLS + policies mechanically; this catches a restore that silently landed with RLS off, a cross-tenant breach the RLS-bypassing superuser reads would never notice

the no-clobber empty-target gate (buildExcessQuery over EVERY FORCE-RLS table, boot-seed rows excluded) is the fail-closed guard that a restore never overwrites provisioned tenant data (TestBuildExcessQuery, DR-005 in-tree proof)
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the restored stack B on the local-production-compose seam; the cloud-VM leg is the operator leg — named, NOT claimed

`palai restore verify` (secret canary focus, DR-006): a stored secret still DECRYPTED under the TARGET stack's master key (secret_decrypt) — the M3 canary against a restore that did not carry the source master key, which would leave every secret row intact but undecryptable (a silently-dead install)

the CLI's openSealed AES-256-GCM open is byte-compatible with the control-plane secret store's seal (nonce||ciphertext); the interop is pinned at the component tier against a real stored ciphertext under two master keys (TestInstallRestoreSecretCanaryTwoMasterKeys, DR-006 in-tree proof)
honest ceiling: at-rest protection is a single master-key AES-256-GCM envelope; the KMS/lease ceremony is E13-H (SEC-001/003)
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the restored stack B on the local-production-compose seam; the cloud-VM leg is the operator leg — named, NOT claimed
